rationale.R1.1: The artifact's thesis and contribution framing appear central to what would be checked in a paper-draft context.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation centrally implies checking whether the argument coheres from the stated benchmark purpose to the later conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes strong claims from reported benchmark and quality results, making support alignment load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: Claims of being first and establishing a system-level crossover require assessing novelty against nearby prior work.
rationale.R1.7: First-systematic-evaluation and community-positioning claims depend on attribution and coverage of relevant scholarship.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of a vague check request must disclose uncertainty about the intended checking scope.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains broad production-deployment and field-redirection claims, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must project responsibilities without executing review, editing, or critique of the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Production-deployment recommendations can influence downstream technical decisions, so harm-aware checking remains active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability between assigned responsibilities and the delegation-artifact pair.
